document: SEBI Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) (India - Row 23)

sections.heading: सेबी ने वर्ष 2015 में बाजार की बुननयादी
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting to SEBI and CERT-In
sections.summary: This section requires REs (Registered Entities) to notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents. Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Non-reporting may trigger regulatory action by SEBI.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates timely reporting of cybersecurity incidents to regulators within specific timeframes, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments involving financial entities and cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: REs must notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents
Stock Brokers/Depository Participants must report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours
All other cybersecurity incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours
Non-reporting by REs may trigger regulatory action by SEBI

sections.source_quote: 1. Any cyber-attack(s), cybersecurity incident(s) and breach(es) experienced by REs falling under CERT-In Cybersecurity directions shall be notified to SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of noticing/ detecting such incidents or being brought to notice about such incidents.
sections.relevance_reason: Breach notification for financial data

sections.heading: Subsequently, SEBI had issued
sections.title: SEBI's 2015 Cybersecurity Framework for Specific Entities
sections.summary: SEBI issued additional cybersecurity and cyber resilience frameworks in 2015 for specific entities including stock brokers, depository participants, mutual funds, asset management companies, KYC registration agencies, and qualified registrar to issue and share transfer agents. These frameworks were aligned with the MIIs circular of 2015.
sections.dtia_summary: This section specifies the entities covered by SEBI's 2015 cybersecurity framework, which impacts data transfer practices for financial institutions handling customer information.

requirements.text: SEBI issued cybersecurity and cyber resilience frameworks for Stock Brokers, Depository Participants, Mutual Funds, Asset Management Companies, KYC Registration Agencies, and Qualified Registrar to Issue and Share Transfer Agents

sections.source_quote: Subsequently, SEBI had issued other Cybersecurity and Cyber resilience frameworks in line with MIIs circular of 2015 for following REs: Stock Brokers and Depository Participants, Mutual Funds (MFs)/Asset Management Companies (AMCs), KYC Registration Agencies (KRAs), Qualified Registrar to an Issue and Share Transfer Agents (QRTAs), Portfolio Managers
sections.relevance_reason: SEBI's 2015 frameworks for financial entities

sections.heading: Further, SEBI has also issued various
sections.title: Non-compliance with SOPs attracts regulatory action
sections.summary: This section states that non-adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) by REs will attract regulatory action as per existing SEBI regulations. The specific actions will be determined based on SEBI's established processes and procedures.
sections.dtia_summary: Establishes that failure to follow internal security protocols can lead to regulatory penalties, which impacts data transfer compliance and risk management in financial services.

requirements.text: Non-adherence to SOPs by REs will attract regulatory action as per extant SEBI regulations

sections.source_quote: 2. Non-adherence to SOP: Non-adherence to SOP would attract regulatory action as per the extant regulations for REs.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulatory action for non-compliance with SOPs

sections.heading: In
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory breach notification and incident response for financial data

sections.heading: सीएससीआरएफ का ढााँचा ननर्ाषररत करने का
sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical. It specifies that low or medium incidents may require forensic reports if root cause analysis is inconclusive or if SEBI directs otherwise. Reports must include root cause analysis, impact assessment, and preventive measures with strict submission timelines.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed forensic reports for high-severity incidents, which are essential for assessing data breach impacts and ensuring compliance with data transfer regulations in financial services.

requirements.text: REs must submit forensic audit reports for incidents classified as High or Critical
REs must submit forensic reports for low or medium incidents if RCA is inconclusive or SEBI directs otherwise
REs must submit final closure reports within 75 days of incident reporting
REs must provide timelines for fixing issues identified in forensic reports

sections.source_quote: 4.1. For all incidents classified as High or Critical, the RE shall submit a forensic audit/investigation report.
sections.relevance_reason: Forensic reports for critical incidents

sections.heading: The CSCRF is standards based and
sections.title: Cybersecurity maturity rating framework
sections.summary: This section defines a cybersecurity maturity rating system for entities, categorizing them into six levels based on index scores ranging from Exceptional (100-91) to Fail (<50). The rating determines the entity's cybersecurity posture and is used to assess compliance with the CSCRF standards. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a maturity rating system that impacts how data transfers and processing activities are evaluated for cybersecurity risks across borders.

requirements.text: Entities shall be rated on cybersecurity maturity from Exceptional (100-91) to Fail (<50) based on index scores

sections.source_quote: 5. Based on the value of the index, the cybersecurity maturity level of the MIIs and Qualified REs shall be determined as follows: SN. Rating Index Score Rating
sections.relevance_reason: Cybersecurity maturity rating for financial data compliance

sections.title: Automated compliance dashboards for SEBI audits
sections.summary: This section requires entities to build automated dashboards integrated with log aggregators for submitting compliance. These dashboards must be available during SEBI's onsite inspections or audits. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates real-time compliance dashboards, which affect how data transfers and processing activities are monitored during cross-border audits.

requirements.text: Build automated dashboards integrated with log aggregators for compliance submission
Dashboards must be available during SEBI's onsite inspections or audits

sections.source_quote: 6. MIIs and Qualified REs shall strive for building an automated tool and suitable dashboards (preferably integrated with log aggregator) for submitting compliance.
sections.relevance_reason: SEBI audit dashboards for financial data compliance

sections.heading: इस ढााँचे के तहत पााँच तरह की ववननयलमत
sections.title: Contingency plan testing
sections.summary: Organizations must establish, maintain, and implement contingency plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery to ensure critical information resource availability and operational continuity during emergencies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once annually.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates contingency plan testing, which is critical for DTIAs as it ensures organizations can maintain system availability and data integrity during disruptions, including cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems
Calculate percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once in a year

cy Plan Testing Measure [RS.MA.S3] Information Security Goal: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems to ensure the availability of critical information resources and continuity of operations in emergency situations.

sections.title: User account management
sections.summary: All privileged users must be identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM) systems, with a target of 100% compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures secure user account management, which is critical for DTIAs as it directly impacts data access control and security during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Identify and authenticate all privileged users in accordance with information security policy
Calculate percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

sections.source_quote: 8. User Accounts Measure [PR.AA.S7] Information Security Goal: All privilege users are identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policy.

sections.title: Cloud implementation VAPT requirements
sections.summary: This section specifies that entities must conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) on cloud implementations and deployments. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates VAPT for cloud environments, which impacts how data transfers and processing activities are assessed for security risks across borders.

sections.heading: सीएससीआरएफ का ढााँचा ननर्ाषररत करने का
sections.title: System maintenance compliance
sections.summary: This section requires periodic and timely maintenance of organizational information systems, including effective controls for tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel involved in maintenance activities. It measures the percentage of system components maintained according to planned schedules, with a target of 100%.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it ensures that system maintenance practices are robust, which impacts data security and integrity during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Perform periodic and timely maintenance on organizational information systems
Provide effective controls on tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used for information system maintenance

sections.source_quote: Information Security Goal: Perform periodic and timely maintenance on organizational information systems and provide effective controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used to conduct information system maintenance.
sections.relevance_reason: System maintenance for data security

sections.title: Media sanitization procedures
sections.summary: This section mandates sanitizing or destroying information system media before disposal, with a target of 100% of media passing sanitization procedures testing. It requires organizations to implement policies for sanitizing media prior to discarding or reusing it.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by ensuring that media containing sensitive data is properly sanitized before disposal or reuse, reducing the risk of data leakage during cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal
Implement policy for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused

sections.summary: This section defines a metric to measure the percentage of physical security incidents that allow unauthorized entry into facilities containing information systems. The target is 0% such incidents. Organizations must implement procedures ensuring secure physical access to critical systems to meet this goal.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a baseline for physical security incidents that could compromise data systems, directly impacting the security of data stored in physical facilities.

sections.title: Planning for authorized access to information systems
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to develop, document, periodically update, and implement security measures ensuring authorized access to information systems. Specifically, it mandates that all users must sign a confidentiality and integrity agreement before gaining system access.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes requirements for user access management, directly impacting the security of data stored and processed within information systems.

requirements.text: Develop, document, periodically update, and implement security measures for authorized access to information systems
Require users to sign confidentiality and integrity agreement before gaining system access

sections.source_quote: 13. Planning Measure [GV.RR.S 5] Information Security Goal: Develop, document, periodically update, and implement security measures for authorised access to the information systems of the organisation.

sections.heading: CSCRF
sections.title: Personnel security screening requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates that 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations must undergo security screening before being granted access to organizational information and information systems. The measure ensures that all critical personnel meet established security criteria for their roles, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires 100% personnel security screening before access, directly impacting DTIA by establishing a strict personnel access control threshold for data transfers involving sensitive personnel.

requirements.text: 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility must be screened before granting access to organizational information systems

sections.source_quote: 14. Personnel Security Screening Measure [PR.AA.S10] Information Security Goal: Ensure that individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations are trustworthy and meet established security criteria for those positions.
sections.relevance_reason: Personnel security screening for critical roles

sections.heading: In order to simplify and streamline the
sections.title: Risk assessment coverage requirement
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of an organization's information systems and assets must be covered under periodic risk assessments. The measure ensures that all critical infrastructure is evaluated for cybersecurity risks, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates comprehensive risk assessment coverage for all information systems and assets, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that all data systems be assessed for risks before transfer or processing.

requirements.text: 100% of organization's information systems and assets must be covered under risk assessment

sections.source_quote: 15. Risk Assessment Measure [ID.RA.S2] Objective of this measure is to periodically assess the risk to organization's IT assets and operations.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory risk assessment for all systems covers data security and compliance

sections.heading: The framework shall be applicable to
sections.title: Third-party service acquisition security requirements
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of system and service acquisition contracts include security requirements and specifications to protect information, applications, and services. The measure ensures that all third-party providers meet adequate security standards before being engaged by the organization, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that all third-party service contracts include security specifications, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that third-party data transfers meet specific security standards.

requirements.text: 100% of system and service acquisition contracts must include security requirements and specifications

sections.source_quote: 16. Service Acquisition Contract Measure [GV.SC.S3] Information Security Goal: Ensure third-party providers employ adequate security measures to protect information, applications, and/or services outsourced by the organization.
sections.relevance_reason: Third-party security requirements for data protection

sections.title: System and communication protection measure
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to allocate sufficient resources to protect electronic information infrastructure. It specifies that the percentage of mobile computers and devices performing all cryptographic operations must be at least 100%. The requirement applies to entities under the SEBI Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to ensure comprehensive cryptographic security across all mobile devices.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates 100% cryptographic operations on all mobile devices, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments as it ensures strong encryption standards for mobile data handling across borders.

requirements.text: Allocate sufficient resources to adequately protect electronic information infrastructure
Percentage of mobile computers and devices performing all cryptographic operations must be 100%

sections.title: Risk management framework implementation
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to implement risk management frameworks that cover 100% of their information systems and assets. sections.heading: The reporting of compliance with
sections.title: Critical assets identification and management
sections.summary: This section mandates that organizations identify and manage critical assets based on their importance to organizational objectives and risk strategy. It specifies that critical systems must be identified as a percentage of all integrated IT systems, with a target of at least 50%.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it requires organizations to identify critical assets, which impacts data storage and processing decisions when transferring data across borders.

requirements.text: Identify critical systems as a percentage of all IT systems integrated with SOC
Target 50% of critical systems identified among all IT systems

sections.source_quote: Critical Assets Identified [ID.AM.S1, ID.AM.S2] Objective of this measure is to ensure identification and management of assets in accordance with their relative importance to the organizational objectives and the organization's risk strategy.
sections.relevance_reason: Critical assets identification for financial data security

sections.heading: यह पररपत्र (सकुषिर) भारतीय प्रनतभूनत और
sections.title: Cybersecurity policy documentation
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to develop, document, periodically update, and implement cybersecurity policies and procedures that describe security controls for information systems. These policies must cover both existing and planned security controls.

sections.heading: Major Change/ Major Release
sections.title: Media sanitization procedures
sections.summary: This section mandates sanitizing or destroying information system media before disposal, with a target of 100% of media passing sanitization procedures testing. It requires organizations to implement policies for sanitizing media prior to discarding or reusing it.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by ensuring that media containing sensitive data is properly sanitized before disposal or reuse, reducing the risk of data leakage during cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal
Implement policy for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused

sections.heading: Market Infrastructure Institutions (MIIs) –
sections.title: Physical security incidents measurement for unauthorized access
sections.summary: This section defines a metric to measure the percentage of physical security incidents that allow unauthorized entry into facilities containing information systems. The target is 0% such incidents. Organizations must implement procedures ensuring secure physical access to critical systems to meet this goal.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a baseline for physical security incidents that could compromise data systems, directly impacting the security of data stored in physical facilities.

requirements.text: Measure percentage of physical security incidents allowing unauthorized entry into facilities with information systems
Target 0% of physical security incidents allowing unauthorized entry into facilities with information systems

sections.source_quote: Information Security Goal: Integrate physical and information security protection mechanisms to ensure appropriate protection of the organization's Percentage (%) of physical security incidents allowing unauthorized entry into facilities containing information systems.

sections.heading: Clearing Corporations
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC

REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact

sections.heading: Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP) –
sections.title: Planning for authorized access to information systems
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to develop, document, periodically update, and implement security measures ensuring authorized access to information systems. Specifically, it mandates that all users must sign a confidentiality and integrity agreement before gaining system access.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes requirements for user access management, directly impacting the security of data stored and processed within information systems.

requirements.text: Develop, document, periodically update, and implement security measures for authorized access to information systems
Require users to sign confidentiality and integrity agreement before gaining system access

sections.source_quote: 13. Planning Measure [GV.RR.S 5] Information Security Goal: Develop, document, periodically update, and implement security measures for authorised access to the information systems of the organisation.
sections.relevance_reason: Access control for personal data

sections.heading: Red team exercise –
sections.title: Personnel security screening requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates that 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations must undergo security screening before being granted access to organizational information and information systems. The measure ensures that all critical personnel meet established security criteria for their roles, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires 100% personnel security screening before access, directly impacting DTIA by establishing a strict personnel access control threshold for data transfers involving sensitive personnel.

requirements.text: 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility must be screened before granting access to organizational information systems

sections.source_quote: 14. Personnel Security Screening Measure [PR.AA.S10] Information Security Goal: Ensure that individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations are trustworthy and meet established security criteria for those positions.

sections.heading: Regulated Entity (RE)12 -
sections.title: Risk assessment coverage requirement
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of an organization's information systems and assets must be covered under periodic risk assessments. The measure ensures that all critical infrastructure is evaluated for cybersecurity risks, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates comprehensive risk assessment coverage for all information systems and assets, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that all data systems be assessed for risks before transfer or processing.

requirements.text: 100% of organization's information systems and assets must be covered under risk assessment

sections.source_quote: 15. Risk Assessment Measure [ID.RA.S2] Objective of this measure is to periodically assess the risk to organization's IT assets and operations.

sections.heading: Regulatory Data –
sections.title: Third-party service acquisition security requirements
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of system and service acquisition contracts include security requirements and specifications to protect information, applications, and services. The measure ensures that all third-party providers meet adequate security standards before being engaged by the organization, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that all third-party service contracts include security specifications, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that third-party data transfers meet specific security standards.

requirements.text: 100% of system and service acquisition contracts must include security requirements and specifications

sections.source_quote: 16. Service Acquisition Contract Measure [GV.SC.S3] Information Security Goal: Ensure third-party providers employ adequate security measures to protect information, applications, and/or services outsourced by the organization.

sections.heading: Risk –
sections.title: System and communication protection measure
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to allocate sufficient resources to protect electronic information infrastructure. It specifies that the percentage of mobile computers and devices performing all cryptographic operations must be at least 100%. The requirement applies to entities under the SEBI Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to ensure comprehensive cryptographic security across all mobile devices.

sections.heading: Secure Software Development Life Cycle (SSDLC) –
sections.title: CSK events closure effectiveness
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to measure the effectiveness of closing CSK (Cyber Security Knowledge) reported events within a timely manner. It specifies that events closed within 15 days of reporting must constitute 100% of all reported events.

requirements.text: CSK reported events closed within 15 days must constitute 100% of total reported events

sections.heading: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) –
sections.title: Cybersecurity policy documentation
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to develop, document, periodically update, and implement cybersecurity policies and procedures that describe security controls for information systems. These policies must cover both existing and planned security controls.
sections.dtia_summary: This section matters for DTIAs as it mandates comprehensive cybersecurity policies, which are essential for ensuring secure data transfers and processing across borders.

requirements.text: Develop, document, periodically update, and implement cybersecurity policies and procedures
Describe security controls in place or planned for information systems

sections.source_quote: Cybersecurity Policy Document [GV.PO.S1] Develop, document, periodically update, and implement cybersecurity policies and procedures for organizational information systems that describe the security controls in place or planned for information systems.

sections.heading: Trusted Channels –
sections.title: Venture Capital Funds Categorization by Corpus Size
sections.summary: This section categorizes Venture Capital Funds (VCFs) based on the sum of corpus across all schemes. Funds with corpus less than Rs. 100 crores are classified as 'Self-certification REs', those between Rs. 100-500 crores as 'Small-size REs', Rs. 500-1000 crores as 'Mid-size REs', and over Rs. 1000 crores as 'Qualified REs'.

requirements.text: Venture Capital Funds (VCFs) must be categorized based on sum of corpus across all schemes
VCFs with corpus less than Rs. 100 crores are Self-certification REs
VCFs with corpus between Rs. 100 crores and Rs. 500 crores are Small-size REs
VCFs with corpus between Rs. 500 crores and Rs. 1000 crores are Mid-size REs
VCFs with corpus Rs. 1000 crores and above are Qualified REs

sections.source_quote: 22. Venture Capital Funds (VCFs) – Table 13: Criteria and thresholds for VCFs categorization
sections.relevance_reason: Purely financial categorization, no data protection/privacy relevance

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting to SEBI and CERT-In
sections.summary: This section requires REs (Registered Entities) to notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents. Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Non-reporting may trigger regulatory action by SEBI.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates timely reporting of cybersecurity incidents to regulators within specific timeframes, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments involving financial entities and cross-border data flows.

Non-reporting by REs may trigger regulatory action by SEBI

sections.heading: KYC Registration
sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical. Reports must include root cause analysis, impact assessment, and preventive measures with strict submission timelines.

sections.relevance_reason: Regulatory action for non-compliance with SOPs

sections.heading: REs Cybersecurity and cyber
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory breach notification and incident response for financial data

sections.heading: REs
sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical. It specifies that low or medium incidents may require forensic reports if root cause analysis is inconclusive or if SEBI directs otherwise. Reports must include root cause analysis, impact assessment, and preventive measures with strict submission timelines.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed forensic reports for high-severity incidents, which are essential for assessing data breach impacts and ensuring compliance with data transfer regulations in financial services.

requirements.text: REs must submit forensic audit reports for incidents classified as High or Critical
REs must submit forensic reports for low or medium incidents if RCA is inconclusive or SEBI directs otherwise
REs must submit final closure reports within 75 days of incident reporting
REs must provide timelines for fixing issues identified in forensic reports

sections.source_quote: 4.1. For all incidents classified as High or Critical, the RE shall submit a forensic audit/investigation report.
sections.relevance_reason: Forensic reports for critical incidents

sections.heading: User
sections.title: Contingency plan testing
sections.summary: Organizations must establish, maintain, and implement contingency plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery to ensure critical information resource availability and operational continuity during emergencies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once annually.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates contingency plan testing, which is critical for DTIAs as it ensures organizations can maintain system availability and data integrity during disruptions, including cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems
Calculate percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once in a year

sections.source_quote:
7. Contingen
cy Plan Testing Measure [RS.MA.S3] Information Security Goal: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems to ensure the availability of critical information resources and continuity of operations in emergency situations.
sections.relevance_reason: Contingency plan testing for security operations

sections.heading: Cybersecurity
sections.title: Cloud implementation VAPT requirements
sections.summary: This section specifies that entities must conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) on cloud implementations and deployments. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates VAPT for cloud environments, which impacts how data transfers and processing activities are assessed for security risks across borders.

requirements.text: Conduct VAPT on cloud implementations and deployments

sections.source_quote: 9. VAPT of Cloud implementation and deployments
sections.relevance_reason: Cloud VAPT for financial data security

sections.heading: Functional Efficacy of SOC
sections.title: Media sanitization procedures
sections.summary: This section mandates sanitizing or destroying information system media before disposal, with a target of 100% of media passing sanitization procedures testing. It requires organizations to implement policies for sanitizing media prior to discarding or reusing it.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by ensuring that media containing sensitive data is properly sanitized before disposal or reuse, reducing the risk of data leakage during cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal
Implement policy for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused

sections.source_quote: Information Security Goal: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal Percentage (%) of media that passes sanitization procedures testing. Effectiveness (Number of media that passes sanitization procedures testing/total number of media disposed or 100% 1.Policy/procedure for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused.
sections.relevance_reason: Media sanitization for data security

sections.heading: Cybersecurity
sections.title: Personnel security screening requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates that 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations must undergo security screening before being granted access to organizational information and information systems. The measure ensures that all critical personnel meet established security criteria for their roles, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires 100% personnel security screening before access, directly impacting DTIA by establishing a strict personnel access control threshold for data transfers involving sensitive personnel.

requirements.text: 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility must be screened before granting access to organizational information systems

sections.source_quote: 14. Personnel Security Screening Measure [PR.AA.S10] Information Security Goal: Ensure that individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations are trustworthy and meet established security criteria for those positions.
sections.relevance_reason: Personnel security screening for critical roles

sections.heading: Review of periodically and
sections.title: Risk assessment coverage requirement
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of an organization's information systems and assets must be covered under periodic risk assessments. The measure ensures that all critical infrastructure is evaluated for cybersecurity risks, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates comprehensive risk assessment coverage for all information systems and assets, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that all data systems be assessed for risks before transfer or processing.

requirements.text: 100% of organization's information systems and assets must be covered under risk assessment

sections.source_quote: 15. Risk Assessment Measure [ID.RA.S2] Objective of this measure is to periodically assess the risk to organization's IT assets and operations.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory risk assessment for all systems covers data security and compliance

sections.heading: Evaluation of cyber resilience
sections.title: Third-party service acquisition security requirements
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of system and service acquisition contracts include security requirements and specifications to protect information, applications, and services. The measure ensures that all third-party providers meet adequate security standards before being engaged by the organization, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that all third-party service contracts include security specifications, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that third-party data transfers meet specific security standards.

requirements.text: 100% of system and service acquisition contracts must include security requirements and specifications

sections.source_quote: 16. Service Acquisition Contract Measure [GV.SC.S3] Information Security Goal: Ensure third-party providers employ adequate security measures to protect information, applications, and/or services outsourced by the organization.
sections.relevance_reason: Third-party security requirements for data protection

sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours

sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.

sections.title: Contingency plan testing
sections.summary: Organizations must establish, maintain, and implement contingency plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery to ensure critical information resource availability and operational continuity during emergencies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once annually.

sections.title: Cybersecurity maturity rating framework
sections.summary: This section defines a cybersecurity maturity rating system for entities, categorizing them into six levels based on index scores ranging from Exceptional (100-91) to Fail (<50). The rating determines the entity's cybersecurity posture and is used to assess compliance with the CSCRF standards. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a maturity rating system that impacts how data transfers and processing activities are evaluated for cybersecurity risks across borders.

requirements.text: Entities shall be rated on cybersecurity maturity from Exceptional (100-91) to Fail (<50) based on index scores

sections.heading: Exemption Table
sections.title: User account management
sections.summary: All privileged users must be identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM) systems, with a target of 100% compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures secure user account management, which is critical for DTIAs as it directly impacts data access control and security during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Identify and authenticate all privileged users in accordance with information security policy
Calculate percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

sections.source_quote: 8. User Accounts Measure [PR.AA.S7] Information Security Goal: All privilege users are identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policy.
sections.relevance_reason: Requires privileged access management for security

sections.heading: GV.SC.S1
sections.title: Personnel security screening requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates that 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations must undergo security screening before being granted access to organizational information and information systems. The measure ensures that all critical personnel meet established security criteria for their roles, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires 100% personnel security screening before access, directly impacting DTIA by establishing a strict personnel access control threshold for data transfers involving sensitive personnel.

requirements.text: 100% of individuals occupying positions of responsibility must be screened before granting access to organizational information systems

sections.source_quote: 14. Personnel Security Screening Measure [PR.AA.S10] Information Security Goal: Ensure that individuals occupying positions of responsibility within organizations are trustworthy and meet established security criteria for those positions.
sections.relevance_reason: Personnel security screening for critical roles

sections.heading: GV.SC.S2
sections.title: Risk assessment coverage requirement
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of an organization's information systems and assets must be covered under periodic risk assessments. The measure ensures that all critical infrastructure is evaluated for cybersecurity risks, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates comprehensive risk assessment coverage for all information systems and assets, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that all data systems be assessed for risks before transfer or processing.

requirements.text: 100% of organization's information systems and assets must be covered under risk assessment

sections.source_quote: 15. Risk Assessment Measure [ID.RA.S2] Objective of this measure is to periodically assess the risk to organization's IT assets and operations.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory risk assessment for all systems covers data security and compliance

sections.heading: GV.SC.S3
sections.title: Third-party service acquisition security requirements
sections.summary: This section requires that 100% of system and service acquisition contracts include security requirements and specifications to protect information, applications, and services. The measure ensures that all third-party providers meet adequate security standards before being engaged by the organization, applying to organizations implementing the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that all third-party service contracts include security specifications, directly impacting DTIA by requiring that third-party data transfers meet specific security standards.

requirements.text: 100% of system and service acquisition contracts must include security requirements and specifications

sections.source_quote: 16. Service Acquisition Contract Measure [GV.SC.S3] Information Security Goal: Ensure third-party providers employ adequate security measures to protect information, applications, and/or services outsourced by the organization.
sections.relevance_reason: Third-party security requirements for data protection

sections.heading: GV.SC.S6
sections.title: System and communication protection measure
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to allocate sufficient resources to protect electronic information infrastructure. It specifies that the percentage of mobile computers and devices performing all cryptographic operations must be at least 100%. The requirement applies to entities under the SEBI Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to ensure comprehensive cryptographic security across all mobile devices.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates 100% cryptographic operations on all mobile devices, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments as it ensures strong encryption standards for mobile data handling across borders.

requirements.text: Allocate sufficient resources to adequately protect electronic information infrastructure
Percentage of mobile computers and devices performing all cryptographic operations must be 100%

sections.heading: GV.SC.S7
sections.title: Risk management framework implementation
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to implement risk management frameworks that cover 100% of their information systems and assets. The requirement applies to entities under the SEBI Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework, mandating that all critical systems and assets must be evaluated, prioritized, and monitored for cybersecurity risks.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates comprehensive risk management coverage for all information systems and assets, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments as it ensures that all data handling activities are subject to risk-based security controls.

requirements.text: 100% of organization information systems and assets must be covered under risk management

Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Non-reporting may trigger regulatory action by SEBI.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates timely reporting of cybersecurity incidents to regulators within specific timeframes, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments involving financial entities and cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: REs must notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents
Stock Brokers/Depository Participants must report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours
All other cybersecurity incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours
Non-reporting by REs may trigger regulatory action by SEBI

sections.source_quote: 1. Any cyber-attack(s), cybersecurity incident(s) and breach(es) experienced by REs falling under CERT-In Cybersecurity directions shall be notified to SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of noticing/ detecting such incidents or being brought to notice about such incidents.
sections.relevance_reason: Breach notification for financial data

sections.heading: The policy document shall be approved by the Board/ Partners/ Proprietor of the REs.
sections.title: Non-compliance with SOPs attracts regulatory action
sections.summary: This section states that non-adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) by REs will attract regulatory action as per existing SEBI regulations. The specific actions will be determined based on SEBI's established processes and procedures.
sections.dtia_summary: Establishes that failure to follow internal security protocols can lead to regulatory penalties, which impacts data transfer compliance and risk management in financial services.

requirements.text: Non-adherence to SOPs by REs will attract regulatory action as per extant SEBI regulations

sections.source_quote: 2. Non-adherence to SOP: Non-adherence to SOP would attract regulatory action as per the extant regulations for REs.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulatory action for non-compliance with SOPs

sections.heading: REs shall have policies (including but not limited to) with respect to asset management,
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory breach notification and incident response for financial data

sections.heading: REs shall formulate a policy for mobile and web applications and associated services
sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical. It specifies that low or medium incidents may require forensic reports if root cause analysis is inconclusive or if SEBI directs otherwise. Reports must include root cause analysis, impact assessment, and preventive measures with strict submission timelines.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed forensic reports for high-severity incidents, which are essential for assessing data breach impacts and ensuring compliance with data transfer regulations in financial services.

requirements.text: REs must submit forensic audit reports for incidents classified as High or Critical
REs must submit forensic reports for low or medium incidents if RCA is inconclusive or SEBI directs otherwise
REs must submit final closure reports within 75 days of incident reporting
REs must provide timelines for fixing issues identified in forensic reports

sections.source_quote: 4.1. For all incidents classified as High or Critical, the RE shall submit a forensic audit/investigation report.
sections.relevance_reason: Forensic reports for critical incidents

sections.title: Cybersecurity maturity rating framework
sections.summary: This section defines a cybersecurity maturity rating system for entities, categorizing them into six levels based on index scores ranging from Exceptional (100-91) to Fail (<50). The rating determines the entity's cybersecurity posture and is used to assess compliance with the CSCRF standards. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a maturity rating system that impacts how data transfers and processing activities are evaluated for cybersecurity risks across borders.

requirements.text: Entities shall be rated on cybersecurity maturity from Exceptional (100-91) to Fail (<50) based on index scores

sections.source_quote: 5. Based on the value of the index, the cybersecurity maturity level of the MIIs and Qualified REs shall be determined as follows: SN. Rating Index Score Rating
sections.relevance_reason: Cybersecurity maturity rating for financial data compliance

sections.title: Automated compliance dashboards for SEBI audits
sections.summary: This section requires entities to build automated dashboards integrated with log aggregators for submitting compliance. These dashboards must be available during SEBI's onsite inspections or audits. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates real-time compliance dashboards, which affect how data transfers and processing activities are monitored during cross-border audits.

requirements.text: Build automated dashboards integrated with log aggregators for compliance submission
Dashboards must be available during SEBI's onsite inspections or audits

sections.source_quote: 6. MIIs and Qualified REs shall strive for building an automated tool and suitable dashboards (preferably integrated with log aggregator) for submitting compliance.
sections.relevance_reason: SEBI audit dashboards for financial data compliance

sections.heading: REs shall follow Plan-Do-Check-Act concept while creating and using the documented
sections.title: Contingency plan testing
sections.summary: Organizations must establish, maintain, and implement contingency plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery to ensure critical information resource availability and operational continuity during emergencies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once annually.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates contingency plan testing, which is critical for DTIAs as it ensures organizations can maintain system availability and data integrity during disruptions, including cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems
Calculate percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once in a year

sections.source_quote:
7. Contingen
cy Plan Testing Measure [RS.MA.S3] Information Security Goal: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems to ensure the availability of critical information resources and continuity of operations in emergency situations.
sections.relevance_reason: Contingency plan testing for security operations

sections.heading: As part of compliance management with respect to CSCRF, REs shall apply following
sections.title: User account management
sections.summary: All privileged users must be identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM) systems, with a target of 100% compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures secure user account management, which is critical for DTIAs as it directly impacts data access control and security during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Identify and authenticate all privileged users in accordance with information security policy
Calculate percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

sections.source_quote: 8. User Accounts Measure [PR.AA.S7] Information Security Goal: All privilege users are identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policy.
sections.relevance_reason: Requires privileged access management for security

sections.heading: The Board/ Partners/ Proprietor of the REs shall constitute an IT Committee for REs
sections.title: Cloud implementation VAPT requirements
sections.summary: This section specifies that entities must conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) on cloud implementations and deployments. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates VAPT for cloud environments, which impacts how data transfers and processing activities are assessed for security risks across borders.

requirements.text: Conduct VAPT on cloud implementations and deployments

sections.source_quote: 9. VAPT of Cloud implementation and deployments

sections.title: System maintenance compliance
sections.summary: This section requires periodic and timely maintenance of organizational information systems, including effective controls for tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel involved in maintenance activities. It measures the percentage of system components maintained according to planned schedules, with a target of 100%.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it ensures that system maintenance practices are robust, which impacts data security and integrity during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Perform periodic and timely maintenance on organizational information systems
Provide effective controls on tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used for information system maintenance

sections.source_quote: Information Security Goal: Perform periodic and timely maintenance on organizational information systems and provide effective controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used to conduct information system maintenance.
sections.relevance_reason: System maintenance for data security

sections.title: Media sanitization procedures
sections.summary: This section mandates sanitizing or destroying information system media before disposal, with a target of 100% of media passing sanitization procedures testing. It requires organizations to implement policies for sanitizing media prior to discarding or reusing it.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by ensuring that media containing sensitive data is properly sanitized before disposal or reuse, reducing the risk of data leakage during cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal
Implement policy for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused

sections.title: Physical security incidents measurement for unauthorized access

sections.summary: This section states that non-adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) by REs will attract regulatory action as per existing SEBI regulations. The specific actions will be determined based on SEBI's established processes and procedures.

sections.heading: Restricted use of removable media and electronic devices
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory breach notification and incident response for financial data

sections.heading: Secure Domain Controllers (DCs)
sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical. It specifies that low or medium incidents may require forensic reports if root cause analysis is inconclusive or if SEBI directs otherwise. Reports must include root cause analysis, impact assessment, and preventive measures with strict submission timelines.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed forensic reports for high-severity incidents, which are essential for assessing data breach impacts and ensuring compliance with data transfer regulations in financial services.

requirements.text: REs must submit forensic audit reports for incidents classified as High or Critical
REs must submit forensic reports for low or medium incidents if RCA is inconclusive or SEBI directs otherwise
REs must submit final closure reports within 75 days of incident reporting
REs must provide timelines for fixing issues identified in forensic reports

sections.source_quote: 4.1. For all incidents classified as High or Critical, the RE shall submit a forensic audit/investigation report.
sections.relevance_reason: Forensic reports for critical incidents

Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Non-reporting may trigger regulatory action by SEBI.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates timely reporting of cybersecurity incidents to regulators within specific timeframes, which is critical for data transfer impact assessments involving financial entities and cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: REs must notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents
Stock Brokers/Depository Participants must report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours
All other cybersecurity incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours
Non-reporting by REs may trigger regulatory action by SEBI

sections.source_quote: 1. Any cyber-attack(s), cybersecurity incident(s) and breach(es) experienced by REs falling under CERT-In Cybersecurity directions shall be notified to SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of noticing/ detecting such incidents or being brought to notice about such incidents.
sections.relevance_reason: Breach notification for financial data

sections.heading: All operating systems and applications shall be updated with the latest patches on a
sections.title: Non-compliance with SOPs attracts regulatory action
sections.summary: This section states that non-adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) by REs will attract regulatory action as per existing SEBI regulations. The specific actions will be determined based on SEBI's established processes and procedures.
sections.dtia_summary: Establishes that failure to follow internal security protocols can lead to regulatory penalties, which impacts data transfer compliance and risk management in financial services.

requirements.text: Non-adherence to SOPs by REs will attract regulatory action as per extant SEBI regulations

sections.source_quote: 2. Non-adherence to SOP: Non-adherence to SOP would attract regulatory action as per the extant regulations for REs.
sections.relevance_reason: Regulatory action for non-compliance with SOPs

sections.heading: REs shall perform comprehensive and rigorous testing of security patches and updates,
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory breach notification and incident response for financial data

sections.title: Automated compliance dashboards for SEBI audits
sections.summary: This section requires entities to build automated dashboards integrated with log aggregators for submitting compliance. These dashboards must be available during SEBI's onsite inspections or audits. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates real-time compliance dashboards, which affect how data transfers and processing activities are monitored during cross-border audits.

requirements.text: Build automated dashboards integrated with log aggregators for compliance submission
Dashboards must be available during SEBI's onsite inspections or audits

sections.source_quote: 6. MIIs and Qualified REs shall strive for building an automated tool and suitable dashboards (preferably integrated with log aggregator) for submitting compliance.

sections.title: User account management
sections.summary: All privileged users must be identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM) systems, with a target of 100% compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures secure user account management, which is critical for DTIAs as it directly impacts data access control and security during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Identify and authenticate all privileged users in accordance with information security policy
Calculate percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

sections.source_quote: 8. User Accounts Measure [PR.AA.S7] Information Security Goal: All privilege users are identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policy.
sections.relevance_reason: Requires privileged access management for security

sections.heading: REs shall also establish processes for tracking patch compliance across all IT systems/
sections.title: System maintenance compliance
sections.summary: This section requires periodic and timely maintenance of organizational information systems, including effective controls for tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel involved in maintenance activities. It measures the percentage of system components maintained according to planned schedules, with a target of 100%.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it ensures that system maintenance practices are robust, which impacts data security and integrity during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Perform periodic and timely maintenance on organizational information systems
Provide effective controls on tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used for information system maintenance

sections.source_quote: Information Security Goal: Perform periodic and timely maintenance on organizational information systems and provide effective controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used to conduct information system maintenance.
sections.relevance_reason: System maintenance for data security

sections.heading: Based on the criticality of the patches, REs shall ensure that patches are implemented
sections.title: Media sanitization procedures
sections.summary: This section mandates sanitizing or destroying information system media before disposal, with a target of 100% of media passing sanitization procedures testing. It requires organizations to implement policies for sanitizing media prior to discarding or reusing it.
sections.dtia_summary: This section impacts DTIAs by ensuring that media containing sensitive data is properly sanitized before disposal or reuse, reducing the risk of data leakage during cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal
Implement policy for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused

sections.source_quote: Information Security Goal: Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal Percentage (%) of media that passes sanitization procedures testing. Effectiveness (Number of media that passes sanitization procedures testing/total number of media disposed or 100% 1.Policy/procedure for sanitizing media before it is discarded or reused.
sections.relevance_reason: Media sanitization for data security

sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical.
