REs must submit final closure reports within 75 days of incident reporting
REs must provide timelines for fixing issues identified in forensic reports

cy Plan Testing Measure [RS.MA.S3] Information Security Goal: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems to ensure the availability of critical information resources and continuity of operations in emergency situations.

sections.heading: Scope of Audit
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.

sections.title: Contingency plan testing
sections.summary: Organizations must establish, maintain, and implement contingency plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery to ensure critical information resource availability and operational continuity during emergencies. Calculate percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once in a year

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours

REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact

sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates contingency plan testing, which is critical for DTIAs as it ensures organizations can maintain system availability and data integrity during disruptions, including cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems
Calculate percentage of information systems that have conducted contingency plan testing at least once in a year

7. Contingen
cy Plan Testing Measure [RS.MA.S3] Information Security Goal: Establish, maintain, and effectively implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery of organizational information systems to ensure the availability of critical information resources and continuity of operations in emergency situations.

sections.heading: User
sections.title: User account management
sections.summary: All privileged users must be identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policies. The measure requires calculating the percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM) systems, with a target of 100% compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures secure user account management, which is critical for DTIAs as it directly impacts data access control and security during cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Identify and authenticate all privileged users in accordance with information security policy
Calculate percentage of privileged access through Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

sections.source_quote: 8. User Accounts Measure [PR.AA.S7] Information Security Goal: All privilege users are identified and authenticated in accordance with information security policy.

sections.heading: Cyber audit
sections.title: Forensic investigation and audit requirements
sections.summary: This section mandates forensic investigation and audit reports for cybersecurity incidents classified as High or Critical. It specifies that low or medium incidents may require forensic reports if root cause analysis is inconclusive or if SEBI directs otherwise. Reports must include root cause analysis, impact assessment, and preventive measures with strict submission timelines.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires detailed forensic reports for high-severity incidents, which are essential for assessing data breach impacts and ensuring compliance with data transfer regulations in financial services.

requirements.text: REs must submit forensic audit reports for incidents classified as High or Critical
REs must submit forensic reports for low or medium incidents if RCA is inconclusive or SEBI directs otherwise
REs must submit final closure reports within 75 days of incident reporting
REs must provide timelines for fixing issues identified in forensic reports

sections.source_quote: 4.1. For all incidents classified as High or Critical, the RE shall submit a forensic audit/investigation report.
sections.relevance_reason: Forensic reports for critical incidents

sections.heading: Incident
sections.title: Cloud implementation VAPT requirements
sections.summary: This section specifies that entities must conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) on cloud implementations and deployments. The requirement applies to all entities subject to the CSCRF.

requirements.text: Conduct VAPT on cloud implementations and deployments

sections.summary: This section requires REs (Registered Entities) to notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents. Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Non-reporting may trigger regulatory action by SEBI.

sections.title: System maintenance compliance
sections.summary: This section requires periodic and timely maintenance of organizational information systems, including effective controls for tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel involved in maintenance activities. sections.heading: Cyber audit
sections.title: Cyber incident reporting and response procedures
sections.summary: This section outlines detailed procedures for reporting cybersecurity incidents to SEBI, including mandatory reporting to CERT-In and NCIIPC for Critical Information Infrastructure systems. It requires REs to document incident handling steps, including adherence to incident response plans, containment measures, stakeholder communication, root cause analysis, and remediation actions. Specific timelines for report submissions are mandated.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides comprehensive incident response protocols with strict timelines for reporting and remediation, which are critical for assessing data transfer risks and ensuring timely regulatory compliance in financial services.

requirements.text: Incidents must be reported to SEBI via email and incident reporting portal within 6 hours
Incidents must be reported to CERT-In in accordance with CERT-In guidelines
Critical Information Infrastructure systems must report to NCIIPC
REs must verify adherence to incident response plans during incident handling
REs must take immediate measures to contain incident impact
REs must communicate incident to all relevant stakeholders
REs must implement measures to control, mitigate and remediate incidents
REs must perform root cause analysis (RCA) for incidents
REs must implement lessons learned from RCA
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in RCA
REs must hire independent agencies for IS audit/forensic audit if applicable
REs must address vulnerabilities identified in forensic audits
REs must submit interim reports within 3 days of incident reporting
REs must submit mitigation measures within 7 days of incident reporting
REs must submit RCA reports within 30 days of incident reporting
REs must submit forensic audit reports within 45 days of incident reporting

sections.source_quote: 3. Whenever a cybersecurity incident is reported to SEBI by RE, the following steps need to be taken: The incident shall be reported on the SEBI Incident Reporting portal and on the email ID mkt_incidents@sebi.gov.in by the RE.
sections.relevance_reason: Mandatory breach notification and incident response for financial data

sections.heading: Critical
sections.title: Critical assets identification and management
sections.summary: This section mandates that organizations identify and manage critical assets based on their importance to organizational objectives and risk strategy. It specifies that critical systems must be identified as a percentage of all integrated IT systems, with a target of at least 50%.
sections.dtia_summary: This section is relevant for DTIAs as it requires organizations to identify critical assets, which impacts data storage and processing decisions when transferring data across borders.

requirements.text: Identify critical systems as a percentage of all IT systems integrated with SOC
Target 50% of critical systems identified among all IT systems

sections.source_quote: Critical Assets Identified [ID.AM.S1, ID.AM.S2] Objective of this measure is to ensure identification and management of assets in accordance with their relative importance to the organizational objectives and the organization's risk strategy.
sections.relevance_reason: Critical assets identification for financial data security

sections.heading: CSK
sections.title: CSK events closure effectiveness
sections.summary: This section requires organizations to measure the effectiveness of closing CSK (Cyber Security Knowledge) reported events within a timely manner. It specifies that events closed within 15 days of reporting must constitute 100% of all reported events.
sections.dtia_summary: This section matters for DTIAs as it establishes a metric for timely event resolution, which is critical for assessing the security posture of data transfers involving third parties.

requirements.text: CSK reported events closed within 15 days must constitute 100% of total reported events

sections.source_quote: CSK Events [RS.MA.S5] Objective of this measure is to mitigate threats upon external IPs Number of CSK reported events closed in timely manner.
sections.relevance_reason: Measures breach response effectiveness for financial data

sections.summary: This section requires REs (Registered Entities) to notify SEBI and CERT-In within 6 hours of detecting or being brought to notice of cybersecurity incidents. Stock Brokers and Depository Participants must also report to Stock Exchanges/Depositories within 6 hours. All other incidents must be reported to SEBI, CERT-In, and NCIIPC within 24 hours. Non-reporting may trigger regulatory action by SEBI.

sections.title: Cybersecurity maturity rating framework
